topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI models including ChatGPT, Claude, and Llama.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free guide that teaches prompt engineering, covering topics from basic to advanced techniques like chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What age group is the 'Prompting for Kids' program designed for?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' program is designed for children ages 8-14, using interactive games to teach them how to communicate with AI.

entries.q: What are some of the advanced prompting techniques covered in the Interactive Book?
entries.a: The book covers advanced techniques such as chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning, which enhance the effectiveness of prompts.

entries.q: What is the self-hosting feature of prompts.chat?
entries.a: The self-hosting feature allows users to deploy their own private prompt library with custom branding, themes, and authentication options.

entries.q: What integrations are available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat can be integrated with CLI commands, Claude Code Plugin, and can serve as an MCP server for AI tools.
